debug_info.goal:
case pos
p : ℕ
inst✝¹ : Fact (Nat.Prime p)
inst✝ : Fact (p > 2)
n : ℕ
hn : 2 ^ (n + 1) < p
i : ℕ
env : Environment (F p)
input : Var fieldPair (F p)
x y : F p
h_input : Expression.eval env input.1 = x ∧ Expression.eval env input.2 = y
assumptions : ZMod.val x < 2 ^ n ∧ ZMod.val y < 2 ^ n
h_holds : ZMod.val y + 1 < 2 ^ n → env.get (i + n + 1) = if ZMod.val x < ZMod.val y + 1 then 1 else 0
this : 2 ^ n < 2 ^ (n + 1)
hy✝ : ZMod.val y + ZMod.val 1 < p
hy : ZMod.val y + 1 = 2 ^ n
⊢ env.get (i + n + 1) = if ZMod.val x ≤ ZMod.val y then 1 else 0

failed_attempts:
case pos =>
      have hx_le_y : ZMod.val x ≤ ZMod.val y := by omega
      simp only [hx_le_y, ↓reduceIte]
      omega

case pos =>
      have hx_le_y : ZMod.val x ≤ ZMod.val y := by omega
      simp only [hx_le_y, ↓reduceIte]
      -- The LessThan circuit with assumption y+1 = 2^n cannot be proven with current spec
      sorry

have hx_le : ZMod.val x ≤ ZMod.val y := by omega
      simp only [hx_le, ↓reduceIte]
      have := assumptions.right
      omega

have hx_lt : x.val < 2^n := assumptions.1
      have hx_le_y : x.val ≤ y.val := by omega
      simp only [hx_le_y, ↓reduceIte]
      have hx_lt_y1 : x.val < y.val + 1 := by omega
      rw [hy] at hx_lt_y1
      simp only [hx_lt_y1, ↓reduceIte] at h_holds
      exact h_holds (by omega)

case pos =>
      simp only [hy] at assumptions h_holds
      have hx_le_y : x.val ≤ y.val := by omega
      simp only [hx_le_y, ↓reduceIte]
      -- When y.val + 1 = 2^n, we need to reason directly about the circuit behavior
      -- The bit at position n determines the output based on whether x + 2^n - (y+1) >= 2^n
      -- Since x.val < 2^n and y.val + 1 = 2^n, we have x.val < y.val + 1
      -- So x + 2^n - (y+1) = x.val + 2^n - 2^n = x.val < 2^n
      -- This means bit n is 0, so output is 1 - 0 = 1
      sorry
